what_it_does: A git diff patch that wires a new top-level 'review' workspace into the canvas frontend. It edits canvas/app/src/App.tsx to add a view-switch (canvas vs review) state and a chip UI toggling between the tldraw canvas shell and a new <Review> region, and edits canvas/app/vite.config.ts to add dev-server proxy routes for /mockups and /design-system.css to the same backend target as /api.
observations: Imports a new Review region component into App.tsx
Adds useState<'canvas' | 'review'>('canvas') to Hud() as a single source of truth for the active workspace
Adds a .view-switch chip with two buttons (◧ canvas, ▦ review) carrying data-ui-ref='ui://view-switch'
Renders <Review/> wrapped in a PanelErrorBoundary named 'review' when view==='review'
Hides the .app-shell via inline style display:none when in review mode so canvas chrome doesn't bleed over
Both views mount inside the same AppContext.Provider so the right-hand-man machinery is shared
vite proxy gains /mockups and /design-system.css routes pointing at VITE_API_TARGET (default http://localhost:8770) for same-origin iframe staging of mockups via the bridge
standouts: Inline comments attribute the design decision to Tim on 2026-06-08 ('a separate workspace, separate from the canvas')
Deliberately router-free: 'Minimal by design — no router, just a mode + a switch chip'
The tldraw board keeps running underneath both views (App() owns <Tldraw>)
conventions: Uses data-ui-ref='ui://...' addressing scheme on the switch control
Wraps regions in named PanelErrorBoundary (defense-in-depth pattern referenced in surrounding comments)
Heavy inline comments documenting rationale and attribution, consistent with the codebase's self-description discipline
notes: A patch that adds a canvas/review view-switch to the frontend shell plus vite proxies for serving the mockup review corpus same-origin.
questions: Does the Review region component already exist at ./regions/Review, or is it introduced by a separate patch?
What backend serves /mockups and /design-system.css on port 8770 (referred to as 'the bridge')?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (patch file; the diff content matches its evident intent of wiring a review workspace)
apparent_themes: Shared machinery across views (same AppContext for both canvas and review)
Same-origin static serving for iframe-staged design mockups
Decision provenance encoded in code comments
intention_signals: Trying to introduce a second top-level workspace (Review) for examining design mockups with the right-hand-man, without restructuring the app, by reusing existing context/error-boundary patterns and a simple mode toggle.
novelty: The 'separate workspace, same machinery' approach — a mode switch that swaps the rendered workspace while keeping the underlying tldraw board and AppContext alive — and routing mockups through the same dev proxy for same-origin iframe staging.
contribution: Contributes the canvas↔review view-switch mechanism and the vite proxy entries that let the Review workspace serve mockups/design-system.css same-origin through the bridge.
summary_for_embedding: Git patch wiring a router-free canvas/review view-switch into the frontend App shell and adding vite dev proxies for /mockups and /design-system.css.
intention_for_embedding: To add a separate Review workspace for design-mockup review that reuses the same AppContext and right-hand-man machinery as the canvas.
